assertions: current_utilization 0.60 is below target
one_month_forecast and three_month_forecast are both returned
every distribution in both forecasts shares the run's simulation_id
current_utilization 0.857 exceeds target 0.85
capacity_position.reserved_not_inbound equals 1 (reserved 3 minus confirmed_inbound 2)
utilization is computed against total_physical_slots, not effective slots (D6)
12 current + 3 confirmed inbound against 13 slots cannot fit
arrivals that cannot physically fit are DEFERRED in the simulation, not dropped -- modeling an impossible ending utilization would understate the cost of being full
risk_probabilities.utilization_above_100_percent > 0
V-10004 (96d), V-10005 (108d), V-10012 (130d) are over 90 days -- 3 of 12, or 25%, at the configured threshold
aging_profile buckets sum to active_inventory_count
projected_unit_count_at_horizon comes from the draws, so future aging is answered rather than inferred
risk_probabilities.revenue_below_target is a direct draw count, not a normal approximation
the result leads with the probability of missing the target
sales_revenue is reported as a distribution, never as a point estimate
forecast_basis.mode is RUN_OFF -- this is the DEFAULT path, since no MCP tool supplies planned acquisitions
forecast_basis.lower_bound_note is present and non-empty (schema-enforced)
ending inventory, utilization, and revenue are labeled lower bounds
FUTURE_ACQUISITION_DATA_UNAVAILABLE is severity INFO, not an error
4 committed inbound units against 2 open physical slots
the conflict is detected from primitives, not from a derived field returned by the capacity tool
V-10004, V-10005, and V-10011 all price below break-even -- 3 of 12, above the 10% threshold
HIGH_PERCENTAGE_BELOW_BREAK_EVEN is CRITICAL but NOT BLOCKING: it is a portfolio finding, and BLOCKING is reserved for §19.1 publication bars
financial_risk.total_exposure_below_break_even sums the shortfalls in dollars
data_coverage.coverage_ratio is 0.75, below the 0.80 confidence threshold
the three vehicles are counted in data_coverage.missing_cost_basis, not silently dropped
they are excluded from break-even aggregates but still appear in inventory counts
sum of per-vehicle P10 revenue contributions is EXACTLY 0, while portfolio sales_revenue.p10 is materially positive (> $25,000)
no vehicle reaches P(sale within 30d) > 0.9, so every marginal P10 contribution is zero -- yet the lot plainly sells several cars in a bad month
portfolio sales_revenue.p50 differs from the sum of per-vehicle P50 contributions by more than $2,000 in either direction
unit_sales, sales_revenue, front_end_gross, and net_economic_value all carry the same simulation_id
this is the regression test for the §14.7 prohibition
expect.note: An earlier draft asserted the portfolio P50 was strictly LESS than the sum of per-vehicle P50s. That was wrong twice over: the direction is not guaranteed, and the comparison was knife-edge because the naive sum jumps discontinuously as vehicles cross P(sale) = 0.5. The P10 form above is threshold-free and demonstrates the same point far more starkly. Measured at the current calibration: marginals sum to $0, portfolio P10 is $51,732.
